extensionDesc.message: Control proxy routing by Firefox Container, no-container tabs, private windows, site rules, and public proxy speed testing.

popupDesc.message: Proxy per container, normal tabs, private windows, and specific sites.

summaryText.message: choose whether each context uses manual proxy, direct connection, blocking, or Firefox. Site rules have priority.

generalDesc.message: Extension default, no-container, private window, and Firefox containers.

rulesDesc.message: Create rules by domain, subdomain, or specific URL.

fastProxiesDesc.message: Fetches from free sources, tests real HTTPS, and saves the best ones.

txtImportDesc.message: Paste or import a list of proxies. The extension detects and formats automatically.

txtPlaceholder.message:
Paste proxies here in format:
socks5://1.2.3.4:1080
http://5.6.7.8:8080
9.10.11.12:3128
(or import a .txt file)

dnsDesc.message: DNS is resolved at the proxy server, not on your device. Recommended for privacy with SOCKS5.

blockInvalidDesc.message: Blocks connection instead of using direct internet when the configured proxy is invalid.

backupDesc.message: Export or import configurations in JSON.

privacyDesc.message: Rules are saved locally. Proxy fetch queries public APIs and testing accesses public HTTPS pages only to measure latency.

testNoProxiesReturned.message: No proxies returned by the public list.

testBestResult.message: Completed: $success$/$tested$ loaded HTTPS. Ignored: $failed$. Best: $speed$ • $ms$ ms.

testAllFailed.message: No proxy loaded complete HTTPS within $timeout$s. Tested: $tested$. Ignored: $failed$.

testAllFailedText.message: All were ignored, failed, or timed out.

applyNoProxy.message: No fast proxy available to apply.

ruleActionDesc.message: Define what to do when the site matches.

ruleInputPlaceholder.message: SOCKS, HTTP or HTTPS — e.g. socks5://host:1080, http://host:8080 or host:port

ruleSettingDesc.message: Define what to do when the site matches.

txtNoText.message: No text to parse. Paste proxies or import a file.

txtNoProxies.message: No valid proxies found. Check format (e.g. 1.2.3.4:1080).

txtParsedSuccess.message: ✅ $count$ proxies detected and formatted. Click "⚡ Test list" to start.

txtAllFailed.message: No proxy from the list loaded HTTPS within $timeout$s. Tested: $tested$.

tutorialHeroSub.message: Learn to configure proxies by context in Firefox in just a few minutes.

step1Desc.message: Firefox isolates sessions into contexts. Each context has its own cookies and can use a different proxy:

step1P1.message: Base for everything without a specific rule.

step1P2.message: Normal tabs opened without a specific container.

step1P3.message: Private browsing. Enable the extension in private windows in Firefox settings.

step1P4.message: Separate contexts you create (e.g. Work, Personal, Shopping).

step2Desc.message: In the "General & containers" section, configure the Extension default first — it serves as the base for other contexts using "Inherit default":

step2Tip.message: 💡 Supported formats: socks5://host:port, http://host:port or just host:port (default SOCKS5)

step3Desc1.message: Accesses the internet without any proxy.

step3Desc3.message: Blocks all connections in this context.

step4Desc.message: Create rules that take priority over containers. Useful to force or block proxy on specific sites:

step4Tip.message: ⚡ Use "All contexts" in the "Apply to" field to make the rule work in any container.

step5Desc.message: Click "Test" on any profile or rule. The extension will:

step5Item1.message: Connect via HTTPS using the configured proxy

step5Item2.message: Display the public IP seen by the server (Cloudflare)

step6Desc.message: Don't have a proxy? Use the automatic search from public sources:

step6Item1.message: Choose the protocol — SOCKS5 is the most anonymous

step6Item3.message: The extension searches, tests real HTTPS and saves working ones

step6Item4.message: Use "Use best saved" to apply the fastest one

step6Tip.message: ⚠️ Public proxies are unstable and can be monitored. Never use for login, banking, email, or sensitive data.

step7Item1.message: Resolve DNS through proxy (SOCKS): DNS is resolved at the proxy server, not on your device. Increases privacy and avoids DNS leaks.

step7Item2.message: Block when proxy is invalid: if a context is configured for proxy but the proxy is invalid, the connection is blocked instead of falling back to direct internet.

aboutCreditsDesc.message: Extension created with care to help Firefox users manage proxies by context simply and powerfully.

aboutCoffeeDesc.message: If this extension helped you, consider sending a Bitcoin as a thank you. All support is highly appreciated!

aboutPrivacyItem1Desc.message: No history, password, cookie, or page content is collected or transmitted.

aboutPrivacyItem2Desc.message: All configurations are saved only inside your Firefox, never on external servers.

aboutPrivacyItem3Desc.message: The extension only accesses the internet when you explicitly request it (testing or fetching proxies).

aboutPrivacyItem4Desc.message: Public proxies can be monitored. Do not use for sensitive data (bank, email, passwords).

rulePrivateDesc.message: Works if the extension is allowed in private windows.

ruleUnknownDesc.message: Internal downloads, extensions or unidentified tabs.
